text.text:
Okayama Kobo Bakery & Cafe is a small Japanese bakery and café tucked inside a hotel lobby, which makes it feel like a hidden stop rather than a traditional standalone café. The space is compact with limited seating, but the interior has a cute and welcoming feel that fits the bakery concept.

The bakery selection is the main draw, with a mix of Japanese-style breads, buns, and pastries that are soft, fresh, and visually appealing. The salt and butter roll is one of the highlights, with a fluffy interior and a lightly crisp exterior that gives it a nice texture. The curry pan is another standout, with a satisfying savory filling and crispy coating that makes it feel like a more complete snack rather than just a pastry. The bear-shaped pastries are also a fun touch and add a playful element to the bakery case.

The drink menu is worth exploring as well, especially the matcha options. The matcha has a stronger flavor profile and pairs naturally with the sweeter bakery items. The specialty drinks, including options like the taro latte, add some variety if you are looking for something beyond coffee. The presentation of the drinks also fits the overall aesthetic of the café and makes the experience feel more unique.

Since the café is inside a hotel lobby, the setup is a little different from a typical bakery. Seating is limited and it can get busy, especially during peak hours, so it may be easier to grab something to go rather than expect to find a table. The staff were friendly and the ordering process was straightforward, which helps keep things moving even with the smaller space.

Overall, Okayama Kobo Bakery & Cafe is a charming spot for Japanese-style pastries and matcha in DTLA. The combination of fresh baked goods, creative drinks, and a cozy hidden-away location makes it a nice place to stop when you are nearby. I would especially come back for the salt and butter rolls, curry pan, and matcha rather than treating it as just a quick coffee stop.

text.text:
Very hidden tiny cafe with GOOD matcha and pastries! This place is located INSIDE the a hotel lobby so it's a bit of a hidden gem.

The matcha is quite good, nice quality and I find the flavor comes through, its not milky or watery. And the cold foam is FLAVORFUL, it doesn't just taste like heavy cream. I got the sea salt and it was very salty, I recommend mixing it because it was almost too salty to drink alone.

Their buns are also good the matcha sunire bun had a crisp frosting on the outside but the bun was soft and pillowy on the inside.

Service is ok but this place is worth checking out for high quality matcha in little tokyo.

text.text:
Ok so this bakery and cafe was such a good find. We were just looking for a cute spot to grab some caffeine and maybe a pastry before we started to explore Little Tokyo. The staff is friendly and the interior decor is cute. It sits in the lobby of the Miyako hotel so there is not too much seating but very nice to sit for a bit if you can grab a table. Both the banana creme matcha latte and matcha seat salt cold brew were fantastic. Great quality matcha and the taste was on point. The furikake salt bread was unique to us so we really enjoyed that. There was also a cute little bear bun that was fluffy and delicious. If you are in the area, definitely recommend stopping in!

Now for my standard comments. The location is clean and well kept. Comfortable indoor seating if you are able to grab a table. Gets very busy and the location is pretty small since it is in the lobby of the hotel.

text.text: Extremely pretty cafe. This is my 3rd time back. I will say the pastries are underwhelming for their price, however the matcha is great. I would say it is overpriced because you have to pay $1 just to change the milk if lets say you are lactose intolerant. You also have to pay for a lot of things in general and the base price is already $6.50/$7.50 for the matcha. Overall it is pretty sweet. It is nice and creamy… overall the matcha blend is nice.

text.text: Features a variety of delicious bakery goods at a reasonable price and delicious beverages including ceremonial grade matcha and limited edition drinks. Would definitely go again to try more of their menu! (Heads up, they are often PACKED on weekends so expect long lines if going then, and there are only a couple of tables so you likely would have to get everything to-go on those days)
